hyperedges.label: Tauri App Architecture: WebView Frontend + Rust Backend + Desktop Shell
Git Processing Stack: Rust Backend + Git CLI + imara-diff
Multi-platform Release Pipeline: CI Workflow + AUR Packaging + Auto-Updater

nodes.label: amend.gif — animated demo of the commit/amend workflow UI in dragit, showing staged vs unstaged file counts, Stage All and Commit action buttons, a dark-themed git graph with commit nodes and branch lines, and a commit entry row displaying message, author, timestamp, and short hash
nodes.author: Alejandro De Cicco

nodes.label: delete.gif - Branch List Delete Demo Animation
nodes.description: Animated GIF demonstrating the branch list UI of the dragit app. Shows a dark-themed panel listing four local git branches: hotfix-1, hotfix-2, hotfix-3, and main. Each branch card displays the branch name with a git icon, tracking remote (e.g. origin/hotfix-1), and last-modified timestamp. The 'main' branch is highlighted with a pink/red border indicating it is the current branch, and has a filled arrow icon next to it. A cursor hand is hovering over the hotfix-2 card, suggesting a delete or interaction gesture is about to occur. The animation likely demonstrates the branch delete feature of the dragit UI.
nodes.norm_label: delete.gif - branch list delete demo animation

nodes.label: Diff Viewer Screenshot (GIF)
nodes.description: Animated GIF demonstrating the diff viewer UI feature of dragit. Shows a TypeScript interface (Settings) with inline diff highlighting: removed lines (red/pink with '-' marker) for fileOpenerApp, openLastOnStart, showToasts fields; added lines (green with '+' marker) for fileOpener, openLastFolderOnStart, enableToast fields. A bottom toolbar displays three view mode buttons: 'Before changes', 'Inline changes' (active/selected), and 'After changes'. Dark theme editor aesthetic.

nodes.label: Diff Viewer UI Component
nodes.description: UI feature showing git diffs with three modes: Before changes, Inline changes, After changes.

nodes.label: Settings TypeScript Interface
nodes.description: TypeScript Settings interface shown in diff: fields fileOpenerApp→fileOpener, openLastOnStart→openLastFolderOnStart, showToasts→enableToast renamed/changed across a diff.

nodes.label: Branch Comparison View - dual-panel local vs remote branch commit list

nodes.label: shortcut.gif – Animated GIF demonstrating keyboard shortcut or drag interaction in the Dragit application
nodes.norm_label: shortcut.gif – animated gif demonstrating keyboard shortcut or drag interaction in the dragit application

nodes.label: Stage File Demo GIF
nodes.description: Animated GIF demonstrating the stage-file drag interaction in the Dragit UI. Shows a file item 'main.tsx' in './src' with a hand cursor hovering over it (indicating draggability), above a 'Staged Changes' panel showing count badge '0' and empty state message 'No files with staged changes found.' Dark-themed Git GUI interface illustrating the core drag-to-stage workflow.

nodes.label: stage-many.gif: Demo of staging multiple files at once
nodes.description: Animated GIF showing the dragit git staging UI. Displays an 'Unstaged Changes' section with a badge count of 7 and a hand/cursor icon, listing files: README.md (./), models.ts (./src/api), branches.ts (./src/api/queries), app.tsx (partially visible). Below is a 'Staged Changes' section with count 0 showing 'No files with staged changes found.' The GIF demonstrates bulk/batch staging of multiple files via the header or badge interaction pattern. Dark theme UI.
nodes.norm_label: stage-many.gif: demo of staging multiple files at once

nodes.label: Square310x310Logo.png – Dragit App Icon (310×310)
nodes.description: Windows Square310x310Logo tile icon for the Dragit application. Design features a stylized letter 'D' composed of a thick purple/blue arc forming the curved spine, two blue filled circles at the top-left and bottom-left endpoints of the arc (representing drag handles or nodes), and a pink/red rounded rectangle with a smaller pink circle on top centered vertically — together evoking a drag-and-drop interaction metaphor. Background is white. Color palette: medium blue-purple (#6366c8 range) for arc and circles, coral/salmon pink (#d9606a range) for the central handle element.

nodes.label: Dragit App Icon
nodes.description: Application icon for Dragit. Features a stylized letter 'D' formed by a thick purple/periwinkle arc, with two purple circles at the top-left and bottom-left endpoints of the arc. In the center of the composition is a salmon/pink circle connected by a rectangular pink block, evoking a git node being dragged — referencing both the 'git' and 'drag' concepts in the app name. The overall palette is purple and pink on a white background.

nodes.label: Dragit app logo: a flat-design icon on a white background. A large periwinkle-blue arc curves from upper-left to lower-left, forming the right half of a 'D' shape. At each open end of the arc sits a filled purple-blue circle (top-left and bottom-left). Inside the arc, centered vertically, is a coral/salmon-pink pill shape (elongated vertical oval) with a darker rose-pink circle at its top, evoking a drag handle or cursor being grabbed — visually representing drag-and-drop interaction. Color palette: periwinkle/medium blue (#6B6BD1 range) and coral/salmon pink (#E07070 range). Style: flat, minimal, no text.
nodes.norm_label: dragit app logo: a flat-design icon on a white background. a large periwinkle-blue arc curves from upper-left to lower-left, forming the right half of a 'd' shape. at each open end of the arc sits a filled purple-blue circle (top-left and bottom-left). inside the arc, centered vertically, is a coral/salmon-pink pill shape (elongated vertical oval) with a darker rose-pink circle at its top, evoking a drag handle or cursor being grabbed — visually representing drag-and-drop interaction. color palette: periwinkle/medium blue (#6b6bd1 range) and coral/salmon pink (#e07070 range). style: flat, minimal, no text.

nodes.label: Dragit app logo SVG — 768×768 icon depicting a drag-and-drop gesture: two circular nodes (one indigo/purple rgb(101,100,219) at top, one coral/pink rgb(239,118,122) at bottom-left) connected by a diagonal coral stroke, plus a large purple curved arc suggesting movement. End-caps and shadows use darker variants of each color (dark maroon rgb(184,96,99) and dark indigo rgb(74,74,159)). The overall composition conveys dragging a git item from one position to another, matching the 'dragit' git GUI application theme.
nodes.norm_label: dragit app logo svg — 768×768 icon depicting a drag-and-drop gesture: two circular nodes (one indigo/purple rgb(101,100,219) at top, one coral/pink rgb(239,118,122) at bottom-left) connected by a diagonal coral stroke, plus a large purple curved arc suggesting movement. end-caps and shadows use darker variants of each color (dark maroon rgb(184,96,99) and dark indigo rgb(74,74,159)). the overall composition conveys dragging a git item from one position to another, matching the 'dragit' git gui application theme.

properties.description: The animation demonstrates triggering a commit action via the Commit button
The GIF contains a commit history row showing commit metadata
The GIF contains a git graph timeline on the left side
The GIF contains the staged files summary panel as a key UI element
The staged files count panel sits directly above the Stage all / Commit toolbar
Each commit row in the list corresponds to a node in the git graph visualization

links.note: The screenshot shows the Unstaged Changes panel listing 5 modified files.
The screenshot is likely used as a context/demo asset for the project (stored in assets/).

links.notes: The screenshot documents the panel's appearance and layout
